text:
When planning a project, setting clear goals from the start is essential. One useful approach is to use SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," a SMART goal could be "increase page load speed by 20% within three months."

To track progress, you can break the project into milestones—these are key checkpoints that show you are moving forward. Each milestone might be linked to a specific deliverable, such as a completed design or a test report. It is also important to check for stakeholder alignment, meaning everyone involved agrees on the goals and priorities. This helps prevent misunderstandings and keeps the team focused on the same results.
